Me as a foreigner living in Australia I've had a good experience as a foreigner in Australia.

At high school I was invited to a party and when I got then a bigger kid told me I was not welcome and started attacking me.

And it's happening all over the world I played soccer at the local club many many years ago.
I was not very good - shin splints, so I took up cycling :-).
4, 5 or 6th division teams, mostly losing.

Chinatown for some Chinese food, with Thai town nearby.
Sushi restaurants dotted around.

German restaurants with sauerkrauts.
Italian restaurants everywhere.
Western sandwiches at every cafe.
Steak, sausages and whatever elsewhere.
It can be hard making a choice, so I make my choices mainly by time and effort.
Something quick and with the least bother Sandwiches don't fill me up enough.
20260309_ThePauseBetweenSpurtsWhenTheWaveStartsButDoesntFullyArriveYet.md: And most are gluten.
Sushi does not fill me up enough.
Eating more does not make up for it.
Steaks are a little too heavy for me.
The chips that come with fish and chips can be unhealthy - some places I can get the rice instead of chips.
So I end up gravitating to rice/rice noodles with a little bit of meat and some vegetables.
Like the food I had growing up in Malaysia Pizza is too hard, having to avoid the gluten and cheese.
At meetup events, I pluck off the olives and salami from the pizzas and chuck the rest away.
I like spaghetti marinara and get this occasionally.
I used to like the creamy pasta sauces, until I worked out that dairy does not quite agree with me.
Periodically I make gf spaghetti with chilli, parsley, shallots, anchovies and olive oil but the hot chilli ends up upsetting my t

So I put in the chilli after more of the ingredients are cooking and the oil has be dispersed I like hot curry like vindaloo, but

We had taken a train to a distant suburb to commence a long ride.
Shortly after we started, without any steep inclines, Larry stopped and said he was not feeling well.

In the initial phase there was some mirroring happening.
They would like similar things that I do, or so I thought so.
This is the early phase of the relationship where some people try to latch onto people pleasers, who do not see through this camou
They would do things like doubt what I say, even for very basic irrelevant things.
This is the gaslighting stage where I start getting confused by the nonsense emanated by these two.
It comes in so gradually that I don't notice until I am intoxicated.
This is the phase like the being in the Lotus Casino in Percy Jackson The problem the two have with me is like a pull-push relatio
They want to be with me because I bring peace, harmony and love.
But they look at me and see the things that they don't have or cannot do, and this stirs them subconsciously.
There is subconscious envy and jealousy.
But they don't want to lose me.
So subconsciously they try to bring me down to a lower level, so they can feel better about themselves.
For me, I did not know the competition, envy and jealousy simmering below and just go about my day, being indirectly put down beca
There's their subconscious behaviour of inadequacy driving this, and my subconscious behaviour of be accepting of peoples faults.
In the end, I had to pull out of this relationship just to protect my mental sanity.
But it took a very long time to recognise this insidious attack on my mental state.
I was their drug and they were my drug.
When we met weekly, they would get their high from being near me.
And I would get my high from "fixing" people, something that I inherited genetically or was inserted into my mind when I was young
